data.title: Kalshi sued in Washington over illegal gambling
data.description:
Online betting platform Kalshi (KALSHI) has been sued by Washington Attorney General Nick Brown over illegal gambling.

The lawsuit alleges that the online betting platform violates the Washington state Gambling Act and Consumer Protection Act.

Gambling under Washington law can be defined as "staking or risking something of value upon the outcome of a contest of chance or a future contingent event."

"Kalshi attempts to skirt state law by branding its betting platform as a 'prediction market,' but whatever Kalshi chooses to call it, Kalshi's operations clearly fall under the definition of illegal gambling in Washington," said the statement released on Friday.

data.title: Apple hires ex-Google marketing exec for AI push
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) has hired former Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) marketing executive Lilian Rincon to help lead its artificial intelligence marketing push, several media outlets reported.
  • Rincon will report directly to Apple's senior vice president of Worldwide Marketing, Greg Joswiak. Her official title will be vice president of Product Marketing for AI.
  • Rincon announced her departure from Google on LinkedIn last week. “Leaving is never easy, and I feel a mix of gratitude and bittersweetness as I close this chapter,” Rincon wrote. “At the same time, I’m incredibly excited about what comes next.”
  • Her previous post hinted at her new role, which touched on helping people adapt to AI.

data.title: Southland outlines $2B backlog strategy and restructures $226M in capital support following legacy project impacts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Southland Holdings, Inc. (SLND) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Frankie S. Renda, President, CEO & Interim Chairman, stated that 2025 results were significantly impacted by several discrete items, most notably a $136 million adverse legal ruling on the Washington State Convention Center project, which "had a material impact on our fourth quarter and full-year reported results."
  • Renda explained that the company recorded a long-term accrued liability due to the adverse ruling, and settlement will be paid by sureties under general indemnity agreements with repayment forborne until March 27, 2027. He emphasized, "I'm extremely disappointed in our financial results for 2025, and I'm committed to providing you with a complete explanation of the challenges we faced and the strategic plan we have implemented to move the Company forward."
  • He detailed a comprehensive capital restructuring, highlighting $116 million of additional capital raised with $14 million secured before year-end and $102 million in Q1. Sureties replaced the senior lender, reducing principal by $14 million and assuming $110 million of debt. Renda highlighted, "the sureties have agreed to waive all scheduled quarterly principal and monthly interest payments through maturity," cutting debt service by approximately $27 million over the next year.
  • The strategic plan includes monetizing idle equipment and non-core assets, settling outstanding disputes, focusing on high-margin core markets, and optimizing the bidding strategy for water-resource, bridge, marine, and tunnel projects.
  • Renda announced new awards in Q4 totaling $118 million in core markets, including a $48 million data-center contract, a $40 million CMAR water resource project in Texas, and a $30 million pump-station project in Florida. The Bull Run Filtration Facility project termination reduced backlog by about $160 million, setting the current backlog at slightly over $2 billion.
  • Keith Bassano, CFO, reported, "Revenue in the fourth quarter was $104 million compared to $267 million in the prior year period. The decline was driven by the revenue reversal on the Washington State Convention Center project of approximately $48 million and other adjustments related to legacy-dispute negotiations, which negatively impacted revenue by $44 million in the quarter."

Outlook

  • Bassano stated, “We ended 2025 with just over $2 billion of backlog, of which we expect to burn approximately 38% in 2026. We are not providing formal financial guidance at this time. Given the magnitude of the restructuring actions underway and the uncertainty around the timing of legacy-project resolutions, we do not believe it would be responsible to provide specific financial targets.”
  • The company will revisit guidance as restructuring progresses and visibility into normalized earnings improves.

Financial Results

  • Bassano reported a gross loss of $193 million in Q4, including $136 million from the Washington State Convention Center judgment and related items, $44 million from other legacy-dispute negotiations, and $22 million from a legacy Civil project.
  • Selling, general and administrative expenses reached $17 million, up from $15.7 million year-over-year, mainly due to bad debt and business transformation costs.
  • Net loss attributable to shareholders was $216 million, a loss of $4 per share, compared to a loss of $4.2 million, or $0.09 per share, in Q4 2024. EBITDA was negative $202 million for the quarter.
  • Civil segment Q4 revenue was $58.4 million with a gross loss of $31.3 million. Transportation revenue was $45.6 million with a gross loss of $162.1 million.
  • For the full year, revenue was $772 million, down from $980 million in 2024, with a net loss of $306.5 million or $5.67 per share and EBITDA of negative $191 million.

Q&A

  • Justin Mechetti, Sidoti & Company: "So last quarter, you highlighted data-center opportunities in the $15 million to $20 million range and $50 million to $75 million range, and recently announced a $48 million award in that vertical. Are you continuing to see opportunities of similar size? And how is that pipeline trending today?" Renda responded, "Yes. We're continuing to see opportunities in that size. We're pursuing jobs in that market, and this job is going really well for us, and we expect to continue to try to grow that side of the business."
  • Mechetti asked about the margin profile of data-center projects versus traditional contracts. Bassano answered, "the data-centers are going to fit into our Civil segment, and we would expect margins to align with the core performance that we've had in that segment thus far."
  • Mechetti inquired if the sale of non-core assets would impact project execution or backlog. Bassano explained, "It does not. So that's going to be a key component to us paying down debt and one of the priorities that we have... We do not anticipate this to have a material impact in the go-forward business from a bidding and execution standpoint."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was focused on growth opportunities in data centers and the execution of the strategic plan, remaining neutral but attentive to operational and financial impacts.
  • Management tone during prepared remarks was direct and acknowledged disappointment, but shifted to confident and forward-looking when discussing restructuring, backlog, and future opportunities.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment shifted from cautious optimism about core business and legacy project wind-down to a more defensive stance due to the adverse ruling and large reported losses, though management emphasized a path forward with capital support.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted, with the company refraining from providing formal targets due to restructuring, differing from the previous quarter's optimism about improved margins and backlog execution.
  • Strategic focus moved sharply toward capital restructuring, debt reduction, and asset sales, as opposed to a prior emphasis on legacy project wind-down and margin improvement.
  • Key metric changes include a significant swing to gross and net losses, revenue decline, and a reduction in backlog due to a major project termination.
  • Management’s tone evolved from optimistic and focused on legacy resolution to defensive and focused on stabilizing the business with surety support and capital optimization.
  • Analysts’ questions remained focused on project pipeline and margin sustainability but showed increased concern about the impact of asset sales and strategic shifts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Major challenges include the adverse legal ruling and substantial charges related to legacy projects, leading to large losses.
  • Management outlined mitigation through surety support, capital influx, debt restructuring, asset monetization, and a narrowed focus on core, high-margin projects.
  • Analysts expressed concern regarding execution risk on new and existing projects, as well as the impact of asset sales on operational capacity.

Final Takeaway

Southland’s management acknowledges a challenging 2025 marked by significant legal and financial setbacks tied to legacy projects. In response, the company has secured $226 million in capital support from sureties, restructured its debt to ease short-term burden, and sharpened its strategic focus on high-margin core markets with a $2 billion backlog. Management remains committed to stabilizing operations, closing out legacy work, and driving future value through disciplined bidding, asset optimization, and targeted growth in markets such as data centers and infrastructure. The company does not provide financial guidance for 2026, citing restructuring uncertainty, but has signaled confidence in its repositioned platform and market opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Autolus reiterates $120M–$135M AUCATZYL revenue target for 2026 while expanding pivotal trial pipeline
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Autolus Therapeutics plc (AUTL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Christian Itin stated that "we had a very good first year of launch with AUCATZYL in the U.S. with $74.3 million in revenue recognized in 2025." He highlighted 67 U.S. centers activated by year-end and positive physician feedback. Itin reiterated 2026 guidance for net revenue of $120 million to $135 million, a shift to positive gross margins in 2026, and an expansion to more than 80 activated centers by year-end. He emphasized recent regulatory approvals in the EU and U.K. and the initiation of the U.K. launch.
  • Itin reported that "the ROCCA Consortium...collected data from all patients treated with AUCATZYL," with 91 patients infused and a 92% overall complete remission rate at day 28 in real-world settings, closely aligning with prior clinical trial data. He highlighted the maintenance of a favorable safety profile and indicated real-world outcomes corroborate the clinical trial experience.
  • Itin outlined progress in expanding obe-cel’s use, including the CATULUS pivotal pediatric ALL study, the CARLYSLE Phase 1 study in systemic lupus, the pivotal LUMINA study for lupus nephritis, and the BOBCAT exploratory study in progressive MS. He noted expected pivotal data for the pediatric ALL study by end of 2027 and for LUMINA in 2028.
  • CFO Robert Dolski stated, "Net product revenue for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $23.3 million, bringing us to a total of $74.3 million for the first full year of AUCATZYL sales." Dolski confirmed a change in revenue recognition policy to align with confirmation of the second dose administration, saying, "this change has no material impact on our existing or anticipated AUCATZYL revenue."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated 2026 guidance for AUCATZYL net product revenue between $120 million and $135 million, expecting a shift to positive gross margins and an increase in activated centers to over 80. Dolski added, "based on our current operating plans...we expect that current and projected cash...will be sufficient to fund our operations into Q4 2027."
  • Itin explained that guidance includes U.S. and U.K. contributions but does not anticipate EU revenue in 2026 and will not break out U.K. revenue separately due to early-stage launch.

Financial Results

  • Net product revenue for Q4 2025 was $23.3 million, with an additional $1 million in license revenue, totaling $24.3 million for the quarter. Cost of sales was $25.3 million, up from $11.4 million in Q4 2024, reflecting a full quarter of commercial activity and additional costs such as canceled orders and inventory reserves.
  • Research and development expense was $35.6 million, and selling, general and administrative expenses were $35.8 million for Q4 2025. Loss from operations was $72.5 million, while net loss was $90.3 million for the quarter. Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $300.7 million as of December 31, 2025, down from $588 million a year earlier.

Q&A

  • James Shin, Deutsche Bank: Asked about the 2026 revenue guide breakdown by geography and EU adoption. CEO Itin responded, "We're not planning to break that out...we do not expect a major contribution yet from the U.K....for 2026, we are not actually guiding to any revenue coming outside of the U.S. and U.K."
  • Gil Blum, Needham & Company: Requested views on the influence of ROCCA real-world data and LUMINA enrollment. Itin said the ROCCA data is "very important," emphasizing the need for continued physician confidence and highlighted ongoing efforts to expand adoption beyond CAR T specialists. On LUMINA, he stated enrollment was "starting to gain good momentum" with additional U.S. centers to come online soon.
  • Salim Syed, Mizuho: Inquired about early disclosure of open-label study data. Itin noted, "we're not planning to actually come early with data from BOBCAT...because I think the data probably would be not interpretable as much as we can get excited about individual patients."
  • Matthew Phipps, William Blair: Asked about BOBCAT and ALARIC differentiation. Itin pointed to early pharmacodynamic data as the focus for 2026 and said fundamental activity in ALARIC is expected to be "driven by the BCMA either predominantly or maybe even exclusively."
  • Yanan Zhu, Wells Fargo: Questioned dose cohort expectations in BOBCAT and use of AUCATZYL in earlier line B-ALL. Itin described BOBCAT as a dose escalation study with early data focused on product properties, and noted that about one-third of real-world AUCATZYL patients had minimal residual disease at inclusion.
  • Emily Bodnar, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about follow-up in CARLYSLE and what would give additional confidence in LUMINA. Itin projected up to 1.5 years of follow-up for the 50 million cell dose and emphasized LUMINA’s more defined population and clear primary endpoint.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in regional revenue breakdowns, real-world data influence, study enrollment, and cost structure, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on execution and data progress.
  • Management maintained confidence in guidance, product performance, and enrollment trends. There was a measured tone with emphasis on operational execution, clinical expansion, and prudent financial management. Itin frequently referenced maintaining confidence and alignment with prior results.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appeared more focused on real-world data implications and pivotal trial timelines, with slightly greater confidence in commercialization and operational scaling.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reiterated its 2026 revenue guidance, with a continuing focus on expanding market share, improving gross margins, and pivotal trials. The previous quarter emphasized a strong launch and initial market leadership, while the current quarter highlights real-world data corroboration and ongoing trial expansion.
  • Management continues to stress operational optimization and market expansion but with more detailed updates on regulatory approvals, pivotal study timelines, and revenue recognition refinements.
  • Analyst focus shifted from launch-related questions and cost dynamics in Q3 to real-world data, regional expansion, and clinical pipeline progress in Q4.
  • Management’s language showed increased confidence in the commercial and clinical trajectory, while analysts maintained a constructive but probing stance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted the lack of early revenue contribution from the EU and U.K. and the need for economically sensible market entry in Europe.
  • Gross margin improvement depends on increased patient volumes and operational efficiencies.
  • The company flagged the inherent variability in quarterly sales due to seasonality and patient scheduling.
  • There is substantial work ahead to expand physician adoption beyond CAR T specialists and to grow market penetration in ALL.
  • Enrollment and data timelines for pivotal studies, particularly LUMINA and BOBCAT, were described as subject to ongoing progress and not anticipated to yield early disclosures.

Final Takeaway

Autolus Therapeutics reported a strong first year of AUCATZYL commercialization, with $74.3 million in 2025 revenue and 67 activated U.S. centers, reinforcing its 2026 net revenue target of $120 million to $135 million and a shift to positive gross margins. Real-world outcomes closely matched pivotal clinical results, supporting physician confidence and market expansion. The company outlined a robust pipeline with upcoming pivotal readouts in pediatric ALL and lupus nephritis, prudent financial management with cash sufficient into 2027, and a strategy centered on operational scaling, physician adoption, and international market access, while acknowledging the need for continued execution to achieve further growth and profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sonida Senior Living initiated with Buy-equivalent at RBC Capital Markets
data.description:
Sonida Senior Living (SNDA) was initiated with an Outperform rating and $39 price target at RBC Capital Markets.

Shares were 0.83% higher at $31.69 during Friday afternoon trading.

"Having recently acquired CNL Healthcare Properties, Sonida has emerged as the 8th largest U.S. senior housing owner," said analysts Ben Hendrix and Michael Murray.

"We are excited to introduce our pro forma model and outlook for this transformative acquisition, highlighting significant added scale, notable earnings accretion, and reduced leverage, which in our view, points to unappreciated value in SNDA's differentiated real estate platform," added the analysts in a research note.

"Our thesis is reinforced by strong demand from aging demographics against a supply constrained backdrop, which should support continued occupancy and rate momentum," said the note.

RBC Capital's rating differs from the average sell-side analysts rating and Seeking Alpha's Quant rating of Hold.

data.title: Bitcoin Crashes, Crypto-Linked Stocks Follow: What’s Behind the Sell-Off?
data.description:
Crypto markets are facing a bloodbath situation, raising a key question for investors: with Bitcoin (BTC-USD) crashing, how far can crypto-linked stocks fall, and what’s the connection?

Over the past 24 hours, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped nearly ~5% to around ~$65.8K, and this crash has also pulled down major crypto-linked stocks like Coinbase (COIN), MicroStrategy (MSTR), Robinhood (HOOD), MARA Holdings (MARA), and RIOT Platform (RIOT).

  1. Coinbase (COIN) fell about ~6% to ~$162.92, with RSI sitting near ~12, reflecting oversold conditions. 

  2. MicroStrategy (MSTR) declined roughly ~5% to ~$126.49, breaking below key support level near $128, with oversol RSI near 10.

  3. MARA Holdings (MARA) fell nearly ~7.34% to ~$7.95, slipping below the $8.20 level due to heavy selling pressure with RSI near 16.

  4. Similarly, Riot Platforms (RIOT) dropped nearly ~7% to $13.05, breaking down from the $14 zone and continuing lower with RSI around 26.

  5. Robinhood (HOOD) fell about ~5% to $67.27, confirming a loss of short-term support. An RSI around 22 signals oversold conditions. 

These stocks may attempt to recover if BTC shows strength, but any bounce is likely to be short-term as per their technical downturn structure. 

From a technical perspective, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is in a clear downtrend after breaking below the ~$96K level, as per the TradingView chart. The RSI is around ~20, reflecting oversold conditions, which means a short-term bounce may happen soon, while the MACD indicator is negative.

In the near term, it may either crash toward the ~$64K support level or attempt a short oversold bounce toward ~$67K. However, unless it reclaims the $68.5K zone, the structure will continue to favor weakness. 

One of the key reasons behind this crypto bloodbath is heavy liquidations. According to the Coinglass liquidation chart, about 130.38K traders were liquidated in 24 hours, with total liquidations around ~$514.72M. 

Most losses came from long liquidations with $34.04M compared to just $394.18K in shorts. This data highlights that bullish bets were hit harder and the price crash got even worse.

At the same time, another factor affected the industry—progress in AI. Anthropic recently launched a Claude Code Security tool to check and fix code issues, reported by Bloomberg. However, a leaked report revealed a more advanced model, “Claude Mythos,” which raised concerns that it could exploit system weaknesses much faster than current defenses.

Data surfaced that it could create risks for technology and financial systems. Overall, this bloodbath situation is due to weak technical signals, heavy selling pressure, and new concerns related to Anthropic's new AI model.

Currently, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and other crypto-linked stocks are in oversold conditions, so a short-term recovery is possible, but investors should note that the overall digital assets industry trend is still weak.

data.title: Meet Wonder: The company that wants to be the Amazon of mealtime
data.description:
Food tech company Wonder continues going down a path of going public. The platform brings together delivery, pickup, dine-in, meal kits, and chef-driven food innovation in one seamless experience, or a mealtime super app of sorts. 

The New York City-based company has hired Gabrielle Rabinovitch to be its new financial officer in a move that is part of its effort to prepare for an IPO in 2027. Rabinovitch's prior experience included serving as CFO at Worldpay and a stint at PayPal (PYPL) as its acting CFO. 

The New Jersey–based “fast fine” food platform was founded in 2018 by entrepreneur Marc Lore, who previously built and sold Quidsi and Jet.com and later ran Walmart's (WMT) U.S. e‑commerce business. Wonder pivoted away from its original concept for a fleet of high-tech vans cooking meals in customers’ driveways  to fixed virtual food hall locations that produce dishes from dozens of licensed and in-house restaurant brands for delivery and takeaway. Lore controls the company alongside a syndicate of growth investors that have provided more than $1.2B in funding.

Wonder has also been active in M&A. The company bought Blue Apron in 2023 for just over $100M, adding meal kits and subscription customers. Then in 2024 it agreed to acquire Grubhub from Just Eat Takeaway (TKAYF) for an enterprise value of $650M. Wonder has also purchased Spyce, Sweetgreen’s former robotics unit, and the restaurant rewards app Claim, strengthening its automation and loyalty stack, and in 2026 moved into direct restaurant ownership with a deal for Blue Ribbon Fried Chicken.

Notably, Wonder has raised over $1.3B across multiple rounds, including a 2025 round led by Google Ventures (GOOG) and others that put its valuation around $7B. Last month, Wonder announced expansion to Texas cities Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston, Austin, and San Antonio, which will bring the company out of the Northeast and Mid‑Atlantic regions for the first time. Looking ahead, Wonder's strategy revolves around continuing to scale an AI- and automation-enabled "super app for mealtime" that integrates first‑party food halls, third‑party restaurant delivery, and meal kits in one interface.

Wonder's geographic expansion and IPO funding could allow it to scale further to the point that it is an incremental threat to major delivery and commerce platforms like DoorDash (DASH), Uber Eats (UBER), Instacart (CART), and eventually broad ecosystems such as Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT).

data.title: Phathom upgraded at Barclays following pullback, Voquezna optimism
data.description:
• Barclays upgraded Phathom Pharmaceuticals (PHAT) to overweight from equal weight following a huge share decline year to date on growing indications that sales of Voquezna (vonoprazan) will ramp up in Q2.
  • The bank raised its price target to $18 from $16 (~67% upside based on March 26 close).
  • Phathom shares are down ~36% YTD. However, analyst Jenna Dividner said that the pullback, along with an expected uptick in prescriptions for heartburn drug Voquezna from Q2 through the end of the year, provides an attractive entry point.
  • "Trends in March are positive, with the most recent week (3/13) topping peak levels in 4Q25," she wrote. "While 1Q overall is trending flat sequentially, we acknowledge that the latest weekly data points are increasingly positive, and a continuation of these trends could shift stock momentum in a more positive direction."
  • Dividner added that the company's targeted commercial approach to gastrointestinal providers rather than a GI and primary care focus "may prove to be a tailwind to drive increased adoption."

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for Super Micro Computer?
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) shares have plunged this month amid news that three people associated with the company, including co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, were facing federal charges of assisting with the smuggling of U.S. AI technology into China. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts The Techie and James Foord for their thoughts on what lies ahead for the AI infrastructure company and its stock.

The Techie: While the indictment news hit the stock hard, the individuals charged are no longer active in running the company's day-to-day operations, and SMCI (SMCI) itself has not been named as a defendant, which matters more than the market's initial 33% panic reaction suggests.

What I'm watching now is whether hyperscalers and enterprise customers quietly start diversifying away from Super Micro Computers (SMCI) out of regulatory caution or whether the sheer cost and complexity of switching AI infrastructure suppliers keeps them locked in.

Not irrelevant to my outlook is the fact that the stock reached a point where bad news is almost fully priced in. At these levels, if customers stick around, SMCI becomes a dirt cheap AI infrastructure growth play. 

The bigger risk isn't another shoe dropping; instead, it's time. Every quarter SMCI spends in legal limbo is a quarter where competitors like Dell (DELL) can chip away at its customer relationships. If management delivers a serious governance overhaul alongside strong Nvidia (NVDA) Blackwell-driven numbers, this stock has a long way to recover. If it's more of the same, patience will wear thin even among bulls. 

For now, I’m maintaining the stock with a strong buy, as I think the worst has happened, and with no dent thus far in relations either to suppliers or customers, up is the most likely direction for SMCI.

James Foord: The path forward for Super Micro Computer (SMCI) is no longer about growth or even profitability, but credibility. The key thing to watch out for is customer behavior. Any sign of hyperscalers delaying or diversifying would pressure revenue.

While a buyout is possible and might help, the governance risk and ties to partners like Nvidia (NVDA) complicate the matter. For now, key competitors like Dell Technologies (DELL) are the big beneficiaries, but this may already be priced in at this point.

data.title: Wall Street slides as Trump delays Iran energy strikes
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages traded lower on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump extended the pause on attacks against Iran’s energy infrastructure into April.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.8%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -1.3% 

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, eight of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with consumer discretionary at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, energy has been the strongest sector.

Treasury yields top multi-month highs. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) added 3 basis points to 4.45%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped 3 basis points to 3.97%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) climbed 3 basis points to 4.97%. 

On the Middle East conflict front, President Trump described talks with Tehran as “going very well.” However, an Iranian official dismissed a reported U.S. proposal to end the conflict as “one-sided and unfair.” 

“The S&P 500 broke below 6,500 for the first time since this Iran war-driven pullback began, and that suggests that stocks want to go lower for the time being and we may very well be headed towards a 10% correction, which would leave us at 6,280. This may end up being one of the most attractive entry points for new money in the market since Liberation Day,” Glen Smith, chief investment officer of GDS Wealth Management, stated. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Entergy Corporation (ETR) climbed +8.6%, and shares of Palo Alto Networks (PANW) fell -6.6%. 

How do you think about gold as an investment? Share your opinion in our user poll.
